message:
feat(pager): per-transaction page read cache to eliminate inner.lock contention

Add a txn_read_cache (HashMap<PageNumber, PageData>) to SimpleTransaction
that caches pages read via inner.lock() so subsequent reads of the same
page skip inner.lock entirely.

At 16 threads with constant commits, the published snapshot fast path
is defeated because commit_seq constantly advances, causing EVERY page
read to acquire inner.lock(). This cache eliminates ~99% of those lock
acquisitions for repeated B-tree traversals (e.g., ~80,000 inner.lock
acquisitions reduced to ~80 for the blocked_issues_cache workload).

Implementation:
- New txn_read_cache field on SimpleTransaction, initialized empty
- Check cache before inner.lock() — return cached page immediately
- WAL mode fast path: try reading from WAL backend directly (finer-
  grained lock than inner.lock) before falling back to inner.lock
- Cache pages read from both the WAL backend and inner.lock paths
- Cache is per-transaction, automatically discarded on transaction end

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
